_doc: Audit of function.kind: <value> usage across reference/patterns.dsl/. Updated after PR #140 Part 3 engine extension (Codex decision). engine_recognized.atomic = the four pure Solidity visibility tokens. engine_recognized.composite = special-cased composites (`any`, `external_or_public`). engine_recognized.dynamic_visibility_composite = the new `_or_`/`|` dispatch added in Part 3 — any composite formed by joining 2+ of {external, public, internal, private} with `_or_` separators is honored, plus the legacy pipe typo `internal|external_or_public` normalized via split-on-`|`. silent_no_op_values now contains ONLY values the engine still rejects: state-mutability hybrids (must be split into function.kind: + function.state_mutability:) and non-Solidity / domain markers (move to an appropriate backend or rewrite/drop case-by-case). See Codex decision points 2 + 3 — these stay fail-loud on purpose.

engine_recognized.dynamic_visibility_composite: Any token of the form '<vis>(_or_<vis>)+' or '<vis>|<vis>(_or_<vis>)+' where <vis> in {external, public, internal, private}. Examples: external_or_public_or_internal, internal_or_external, internal_or_private_or_public, internal|external_or_public.

affected_files.rationale: mixes visibility + state-mutability — split into separate function.kind: + function.state_mutability: predicates, or add an explicit rationale and widen intentionally
unrecognized token(s) — non-Solidity / domain marker; move to an appropriate backend or rewrite/drop case-by-case
